questions.question: Convert the statement 'All students who study computer science are interested in artificial intelligence' to predicate logic.
keyPoints: Define predicate for studies: Studies(x, CS)
Define predicate for interested: InterestedIn(x, AI)
Proper implication structure: ∀x (Student(x) ∧ Studies(x, CS)) → InterestedIn(x, AI)

questions.question: Explain the process of converting a predicate logic formula to clause form, detailing each step with examples.
keyPoints: Eliminate implications using equivalence P → Q ≡ ¬P ∨ Q
Move negations inward using De Morgan's laws
Standardize variables to avoid naming conflicts
Convert to prenex form with all quantifiers at the beginning
